target.title: fix(qqbot): gate fallback approval buttons
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the QQBot approval fallback path so unconfigured `execApprovals` no longer grants explicit approval authority to native button callbacks.

## Changes

- Export the implicit same-chat approval marker through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-auth-runtime`.
- Mark QQBot no-`execApprovals` fallback authorization as implicit instead of explicit.
- Gate QQBot native approval button fallback clicks through the existing explicit command-style actor authorization before resolving approvals.
- Add QQBot regression tests, SDK subpath/API baseline updates, and docs for custom/native approval auth.

## Validation

Behavior addressed: QQBot native approval fallback buttons no longer resolve approvals for actors that are not explicitly authorized when `channels.qqbot.execApprovals` is absent.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Node 22.22.2; QQBot behavior covered by production-code Vitest tests and the QQBot extension test lane.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/exec-approvals.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/interaction-handler.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-auth-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts src/infra/channel-approval-auth.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts`; `node scripts/test-extension.mjs qqbot`; `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`; `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md`; `node scripts/check-docs-i18n-glossary.mjs`; `node scripts/build-all.mjs`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local ...`.

Evidence after fix: Targeted Vitest passed 6 files / 56 tests; QQBot extension lane passed 53 files / 434 tests; Plugin SDK API baseline, docs format, docs MDX, i18n glossary, build, and final autoreview passed.

Observed result after fix: Unauthorized QQBot fallback button actors are acknowledged with `You are not authorized to approve this request.` and `resolveApproval` is not called; configured approvers and explicitly command-authorized fallback actors still resolve approvals.

What was not tested: Live QQ platform delivery was not exercised. Crabbox/Testbox broad remote proof was unavailable because no usable `crabbox` binary was present in this environment.

Known proof gap: `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base HEAD^ --head HEAD` fails in the repo-wide extension-test typecheck on unchanged `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts` because its mock lacks `getSession`; the QQBot lane and build passed.

## Notes

- AI-assisted PR; sanitized transcript logs were not included.
- `CHANGELOG.md` was intentionally not updated.

[CODEX BEHAVIORAL PROOF]

Behavior addressed: QQBot native fallback approval buttons no longer resolve approvals for unconfigured `execApprovals` unless the button actor is explicitly command-authorized by the active QQBot config; stale gateway account snapshots are ignored for this fallback decision.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout synced to final live PR head `698ef6ab`. The native-callback runtime proof was collected on PR head `805ce149bc` using production `GatewayConnection`, QQBot event dispatch, `createInteractionHandler`, `authorizeQQBotApprovalAction`, and the same slash-command authorization resolver path passed by the QQBot gateway. The QQBot approval behavior files are unchanged between `805ce149bc` and `698ef6ab`; the final head also contains the CI baseline test expectation fixes. The QQ network was represented by a local WebSocket gateway plus redacted REST stubs for token, gateway URL, and interaction ACK endpoints because no live QQBot credentials were present in this environment.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --name-only 805ce149bccfd67cb3007dc2e6f1d3815842fb08..698ef6abb017e5f961fad134b460ddced16afe55 -- extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/sdk-adapter.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/interaction-handler.ts extensions/qqbot/src/exec-approvals.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-auth-helpers.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-auth-runtime.ts` returned no files. `git diff --name-only f10e2f10da..698ef6abb017e5f961fad134b460ddced16afe55 -- src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/commands/agent/session.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/sdk-adapter.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/interaction-handler.ts extensions/qqbot/src/exec-approvals.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-auth-helpers.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-auth-runtime.ts` also returned no files, proving the final force-refresh did not alter the locally validated files. Runtime proof used `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with an inline harness that starts a local QQ gateway protocol WebSocket, patches QQ REST calls with redacted responses, injects two real `INTERACTION_CREATE` approval-button payloads, and records interaction ACKs plus `resolveApproval` calls. Local validation before the final force-refresh ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/commands/agent/session.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/exec-approvals.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/interaction-handler.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/command-auth.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-auth-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts src/infra/channel-approval-auth.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts`; `node scripts/test-extension.mjs qqbot`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`. The PR `Real behavior proof` check is green on `698ef6ab`.

Evidence after fix: Runtime log showed `[qqbot-api] ack interaction=interaction-unauthorized content=You are not authorized to approve this request.`, `[assert] stale account allowFrom ignored; unauthorized fallback actor rejected; resolveApproval calls=0`, `[platform] resolveApproval approvalId=exec:dddd1111eeee2222 decision=allow-once`, and `[result] QQBot native callback behavior proof passed`. Targeted ACP/session Vitest passed 2 files / 100 tests; oxlint passed; QQBot approval Vitest passed 7 files / 68 tests; QQBot extension lane passed 54 files / 441 tests; extension TypeScript passed. The final PR head has no file differences in those validated QQBot approval/test surfaces compared with the locally validated head.

Observed result after fix: An injected attacker callback from `ATTACKER_OPENID` was rejected even though the simulated stale gateway account snapshot still listed that actor. The active-config owner callback from `OWNER_OPENID` ACKed cleanly and called `resolveApproval` for `exec:dddd1111eeee2222` with `allow-once`. The final force-refresh only preserved the validated test expectation fixes and did not change QQBot approval behavior files.

What was not tested: A real QQ platform callback with live QQ credentials was not exercised. Crabbox/Testbox was also unavailable from this checkout: `command -v crabbox` found no usable binary and `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --help` failed its binary sanity check.
target.author: eleqtrizit
